stories.headline: Israel escalates attacks on medics in Lebanon with deadly ‘quadruple tap’ Lebanese health ministry says killing of 91 healthcare workers shows ‘total disregard’ for international law
stories.summary: Israel has escalated attacks on medics in Lebanon, with the Lebanese health ministry reporting 91 healthcare workers killed, showing a "total disregard" for international law. The ministry accused Israel of deliberately targeting ambulance crews, stating that paramedics have become direct targets, pursued relentlessly in a blatant violation of humanitarian law. At least 54 health workers are among over 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups claim first responders are being targeted, contributing to a health crisis and displacement in southern Lebanon. The attacks on hospitals, medical centers, and healthcare workers are exacerbating the crisis, with the Lebanese health ministry condemning the actions as a violation of international norms and principles. The situation continues to deteriorate, with the death toll and displacement numbers increasing.

stories.headline: Israel, Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, Trump says A 10-day ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon was announced by U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday, six weeks into a renewed war with Hezbollah that ha
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, effective at 5 p.m. ET. The agreement was reached after Trump spoke with Israeli Premier Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun. The truce pauses fighting between Israeli forces and the militant group Hezbollah, starting at midnight on Friday morning in Lebanon. Trump stated that Lebanon and Israel will work towards a longer-term deal, with Lebanon agreeing to "take care of Hezbollah." The 10-day ceasefire aims to remove a major obstacle to peace, six weeks into the renewed war between Israel and Hezbollah. The agreement marks a temporary halt in hostilities, with the goal of achieving a more permanent resolution in the future. The ceasefire is set to last for 10 days, beginning on the announced date.

stories.headline: No date set for US-Iran talks, as Pakistan pushes to keep diplomacy alive Pakistan’s prime minister and army chief are both travelling to key players in the war, attempting to keep talks going.
stories.summary: Pakistan's prime minister and army chief are traveling to key players in the US-Iran conflict to keep diplomacy alive. The army chief has landed in Tehran with a high-level military delegation, aiming to help renew talks between the US and Iran. The two sides failed to reach a peace agreement in Islamabad over the weekend, with US representative JD Vance citing it as "bad news for Iran". Pakistan has been acting as an intermediary between Washington and Tehran, and senior officials are expected in Tehran to continue efforts to keep the diplomacy going. The prime minister is also meeting with the Saudi crown prince to discuss the situation with Iran. No date has been set for new US-Iran talks, but Pakistan's efforts aim to prevent a breakdown in negotiations.

stories.headline: The European Investment Bank Group, the European Commission and IFC back Biovac to build Africa's... €75 million EIB Group quasi-equity investment to support Africa's first end-to-end multi-vaccine manufacturing facility in...
stories.summary: The European Investment Bank Group, European Commission, and International Finance Corporation are partnering to support Biovac's development of Africa's first end-to-end multi-vaccine manufacturing facility in South Africa. The EIB Group is providing a €75 million quasi-equity investment, which will be supplemented by $20 million in senior financing from the IFC. The investment is backed by a European Commission EFSD+ guarantee under the Human Development Accelerator. This landmark public-private financing effort aims to strengthen Africa's health security and vaccine self-reliance. The facility, to be built in Cape Town, will enhance local vaccine production and health resilience across the continent. The project is part of the EU's Global Gateway strategy, which seeks to promote sustainable and equitable development in partner countries. The investment will enable Biovac to expand its vaccine manufacturing capabilities and improve access to vaccines in Africa.

stories.headline: Apple Doesn't Tell You This: 7 iPhone Settings You Should Change ASAP
stories.summary: Apple experts have identified 7 iPhone settings that users should change immediately to improve their experience, security, and privacy. These settings, which are not explicitly mentioned by Apple, can drain battery and reduce performance. With the release of iOS 18, users can now customize the two shortcuts at the bottom of the Lock Screen, previously limited to the flashlight and Camera app. Changing these settings can bring a better experience and enhance security and privacy. The recommended changes include tweaking hidden settings that can impact iPhone performance and battery life. By adjusting these settings, users can optimize their iPhone's functionality and overall user experience. The specific settings to change are not detailed by Apple, but experts recommend reviewing and adjusting them to improve their iPhone's performance.

stories.headline: Galaxy S27 Ultra may perform on par with desktops, and not just because of a 2nm chip
stories.summary: The upcoming Samsung Galaxy S27 Ultra is expected to perform on par with desktops, thanks to its powerful chipset and advanced storage. The device will be powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 or Pro chipset, built on TSMC's 2nm process, offering a 12% performance improvement and 25% power reduction. Additionally, the Galaxy S27 Ultra may feature UFS 5.0 storage, which would significantly enhance the device's performance. The storage is expected to have the same capacity as its predecessor, but with improved speed. The combination of the advanced chipset and storage could make the Galaxy S27 Ultra a highly efficient and powerful device, rivaling the performance of desktops. The device will also feature the latest LPDDR6 RAM, further boosting its performance capabilities.

stories.headline: Google Opens Gemma 4 Under Apache 2.0 with Multimodal and Agentic Capabilities
stories.summary: Google has released Gemma 4, a family of four open multimodal models, under the Apache 2.0 license. The models include a 26B MoE, 31B Dense, and two edge variants, with the 31B dense model scoring 89.2% on AIME 2026. This surpasses the performance of Gemma 3 by four times. The Gemma 4 models are designed to run directly on user hardware, including phones, desktops, and IoT boards. The Apache 2.0 license offers full commercial freedom, allowing creators and developers to build on the models without restrictions. The release of Gemma 4 is expected to significantly impact the development of agentic AI workflows, with the models available for use on a range of devices, from Raspberry Pi to H100. Google DeepMind launched Gemma 4 on April 2, 2026, making the models available for commercial and personal use.

stories.headline: Temporal Power With Spiking Networks Can quantum algorithms learn from data that unfolds over time as effectively as they handle static information?
stories.summary: Researchers have developed a quantum algorithm that can effectively learn from data that unfolds over time, addressing a limitation of current quantum machine learning (QML) pipelines. Most QML pipelines rely on static encodings, which limit their ability to handle temporal information. The new approach uses a combination of a variational quantum circuit (VQC) and a deep neural network (DNN), and employs spike-based data representation as an effective encoding mechanism. This allows the algorithm to handle spatial and temporal information, making it suitable for processing spatio-temporal event-based data. The framework, called SQSNN, has been shown to maintain high performance even with relatively few spikes, outperforming other methods such as QLIF-SNN. This breakthrough has the potential to highlight quantum advantages in handling complex and real-world datasets. The algorithm's effectiveness is demonstrated through iterative optimization processes on NISQ devices.

stories.headline: Alex Manninger, former Arsenal goalkeeper, dies in traffic accident
stories.summary: Alex Manninger, a former Arsenal and Austria international goalkeeper, has died in a traffic accident at the age of 48. The incident occurred at a level crossing when his car was struck by a train at around 08:20 local time. Manninger played for several European clubs, including Juventus and Red Bull Salzburg, and helped Arsenal win the Double in 1998. His former clubs, including Salzburg and Juventus, have paid tribute to him on social media, expressing their condolences to his family and friends. Salzburg confirmed the news, stating that they mourn the loss of their former goalkeeper and are thinking of his loved ones. Manninger's death has been met with an outpouring of tributes from the football community.

stories.headline: Moises Caicedo agrees lucrative new contract at Chelsea
stories.summary: Moises Caicedo has agreed to a lucrative new contract at Chelsea, securing his long-term future at the club. The Ecuadorian midfielder's new deal will run until June 2033, with a significant improvement in his salary. Caicedo has verbally agreed to the contract, with official confirmation expected shortly. The agreement is a major boost for Chelsea, as they seek to reward their high-performing players. Caicedo's commitment to the club aligns with Chelsea's vision for the future, and the new contract reflects his importance to the team. The midfielder will continue to play a key role at Stamford Bridge, with his new deal cementing his position as a key player for the club. The contract extension is a significant development for Chelsea, as they look to build a strong squad for the long term.

stories.headline: Heat, Hornets react to NBA ruling on uncalled LaMelo Ball foul, and how he'll be eligible to play
stories.summary: The NBA has ruled that LaMelo Ball's physical contact with Bam Adebayo was a flagrant foul worthy of ejection. The incident occurred during a recent matchup between the Charlotte Hornets and the Miami Heat. Ball has been fined $35,000 for the play, which injured Adebayo. Despite the severity of the ruling, the Heat organization has shown minimal public reaction. In contrast, the Hornets expressed relief that the decision did not include a suspension, making Ball eligible to play. Adebayo has called for the league to review how plays like this can be assessed going forward. The ruling has brought attention to the need for consistent officiating and player safety in the NBA. Ball's eligibility to play is a positive outcome for the Hornets, while the Heat and Adebayo focus on moving forward from the incident.

stories.headline: 2026 World Cup: Canadian High Commissioner Says Tickets Do Not Guarantee Visas
stories.summary: The Canadian High Commissioner to Ghana, Myriam Montrat, has warned that having a FIFA World Cup match ticket does not guarantee a visa to enter Canada. This comes as the 2026 World Cup approaches, with Canada, the US, and Mexico jointly hosting the tournament. The US Travel Association notes that while the World Cup has economic potential, safety concerns and entry barriers may limit its benefits. Fans are advised to plan ahead due to high global demand for tickets. Verified FIFA tickets can ensure safe entry and guaranteed seating, but travelers must still obtain necessary visas. The warning aims to prevent last-minute issues for prospective travelers, emphasizing that a match ticket is not a guarantee of entry into Canada or other host countries. Fans should research and comply with visa requirements to avoid travel disruptions.

stories.headline: French President Macron calls PM Modi ahead of France-UK meet on Strait of Hormuz Macron and Modi discuss restoring navigation in Strait of Hormuz ahead of France-UK meet amid rising West Asia tensions
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron called Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi to discuss the situation in the Strait of Hormuz ahead of a France-UK meeting. The two leaders agreed on the need to ensure safety and freedom of navigation in the Strait. This conversation comes amid rising tensions in West Asia. France and the UK are set to co-host a summit to discuss a multinational plan to safeguard shipping in the region. PM Modi and President Macron also discussed the escalating situation in West Asia, emphasizing the need for dialogue and diplomacy to restore peace. They expressed commitment to shared coordination towards this goal. The call is part of a series of high-level conversations PM Modi has held with global leaders this week to address the situation in the region.

stories.headline: Vedanta power plant blast: FIR filed against chairman Anil Agrawal, plant head; death toll rises to 20
stories.summary: An FIR has been filed against Vedanta Group Chairman Anil Agarwal and plant head Devendra Patel in connection with the blast at the Vedanta power plant in Chhattisgarh's Sakti district. The blast occurred on April 14, resulting in the deaths of 20 people and injuring several others. Eight to ten individuals, including Agarwal and Patel, have been named in the FIR, with more names to be added if found responsible during the investigation. Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Bhupesh Baghel has announced an ex-gratia of Rs 5 lakh for the kin of the deceased. The death toll has risen to 20, with the investigation into the incident ongoing. The FIR is a significant development in the case, holding top officials accountable for the tragic incident.

stories.headline: Amit Shah announces new share of southern states in Lok Sabha amid concerns
stories.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced the new seat share of southern states in the Lok Sabha after the proposed expansion to accommodate women's reservation. Tamil Nadu will receive 20 additional seats, Kerala 10, Telangana 9, and Andhra Pradesh 13. This announcement comes amid concerns that the expansion would lead to a decline in representation for southern states. Shah clarified that the expansion will protect regional representation, with a special focus on southern states, following a delimitation exercise. The announcement follows an earlier statement by Union Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal that all states' Lok Sabha seats would be increased by 50% after the new delimitation exercise. Shah's clarification aims to address concerns and provide a clear picture of the new seat allocation for southern states in the Lok Sabha. The expansion is part of the proposed women's reservation initiative.

stories.headline: Tamil Nadu 59, Karnataka 42: Amit Shah shares breakdown of Lok Sabha states after delimitation
stories.summary: Amit Shah clarified the projected increase in Lok Sabha seats for southern states after delimitation. Karnataka's seats will rise from 28 to 42, Andhra Pradesh from 25 to 38, Telangana from 17 to 26, and Tamil Nadu from 39 to 59. The total seats for the five southern states - Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana - will increase from 129 to 195. Shah's statement came after Union Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal said all states' Lok Sabha seats would increase by 50%. However, Shah explained that the representation of states like Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana will remain almost the same proportionally. The delimitation exercise aims to readjust the number of Lok Sabha seats based on population changes. The proposed increases will take effect after the delimitation process is completed.

stories.headline: Gulf states biggest losers, angry at US for starting Iran war: Former State Dept negotiator Aaron David Miller
stories.summary: Former US State Department negotiator Aaron David Miller stated that Gulf nations are the biggest losers in the US-Iran conflict, with growing anger towards Washington for initiating the war. Miller believes the Trump administration misjudged Iran's response to US strikes. The US appears to be losing control in the conflict, while Russian President Putin is emerging as the biggest winner. Despite a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, tensions remain high, with Israel launching its largest attacks yet on Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon. Miller criticized Washington's assumptions about the pace and substance of talks with Iran, arguing that the US underestimated Iran's position. The conflict has led to increased instability in the region, with Gulf states bearing the brunt of the consequences. Miller's comments come as the region remains on high alert.

stories.headline: Geopolitics to further fuel Hong Kong mining fundraising amid ‘supercycle’, experts say
stories.summary: Geopolitical tensions are expected to drive fundraising demand in Hong Kong's mining sector amid a commodities "supercycle". Industry experts predict that the current rally, fueled by geopolitics and social media signals, will continue to be unpredictable and risky for investors. The situation is complicated by Beijing's tightened scrutiny of Chinese companies listing in Hong Kong, which may impact the city's IPO pipeline. Meanwhile, the ongoing war in the Middle East is contributing to rising fuel prices, with Hong Kong's transport sector considering a temporary fuel surcharge. The combination of these factors is creating a complex environment for mining companies seeking to raise funds in Hong Kong, with national security concerns potentially complicating dealmaking. Despite the challenges, experts believe that the "supercycle" will continue to drive demand for mining sector fundraising.

stories.headline: Why are oil and gas prices up today, and will Brent, US WTI crude futures, Dutch and British gas rates continue to rise or drop again?
stories.summary: Oil and gas prices have surged due to escalating tensions with Iran and potential disruptions to the Hormuz Strait, a key oil supply route. May WTI crude oil prices rose 11.94% to $111.54 in the week ending April 3. The number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the US has increased. Energy markets are reacting to Middle East conflict risks, falling US inventories, and shifting gas supply signals. Rising US crude inventories can signal weaker demand and push prices down, but current trends indicate continued price increases. Wells Fargo suggests it may be time to take profits on energy despite higher oil price forecasts. The situation is being closely monitored, with market analysts and experts providing insights on the potential future direction of oil and gas prices.

stories.headline: The original clickbait king
stories.summary: William Randolph Hearst, a 19th-century newspaper tycoon, is considered the original clickbait king. He knew how to attract readers with sensationalized and misleading stories, exploiting the "curiosity gap" to boost sales. This tactic, now known as clickbait, is still used today to entice users to click on links and view online content. Clickbait typically involves deceptive or sensationalized headlines and thumbnails designed to grab attention. Recently, there have been efforts to combat clickbait, with some platforms trialing tools to identify original authors of content and allocate revenue to them, incentivizing high-quality, original material. Despite its negative connotation, clickbait remains a widely used tactic, with many users admitting to clicking on spicy or provocative stories. Hearst's legacy continues to influence the way online content is presented and consumed today.

stories.headline: Is the timing of delimitation bill BJP's gamble for West Bengal polls?
stories.summary: The BJP-led Centre's introduction of the delimitation bill is being seen as a strategic move ahead of the West Bengal polls. The bill aims to redraw constituency boundaries, potentially benefiting the BJP in the 2029 Lok Sabha elections. Opposition parties suspect the move is a ploy to gain an advantage, with some states experiencing a non-uniform increase in seats. In West Bengal, the number of seats is set to increase from 42 to 52-53, whereas other states will see a more significant rise. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has criticized the move, alleging it is an attempt to divide the state and implement the National Register of Citizens (NRC). The opposition plans to discuss the bill at a party-level meeting, amid concerns over its timing and potential impact on the electoral landscape. The bill's introduction has sparked controversy and debate.

stories.headline: Parliament Special Session 2026 Live: 207 MPs say 'Yes', 126 MPs say 'No' On Introduction Of 3 Key Bills
stories.summary: The Indian Parliament's special session saw 207 MPs vote in favor of introducing three key bills, including the Constitution (Amendment) Bill, while 126 MPs voted against. Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the Lok Sabha, warning those opposing the women's reservation bill that they will face consequences. The bills aim to operationalize the 2023 legislation providing 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies. The government introduced the bills during the special three-day session, which began on April 16. Opposition MPs protested by tearing copies of the bills in the Lok Sabha. The session will focus on women's reservation and delimitation, with the government expecting to pass the amendment bills. PM Modi is scheduled to speak in the Lok Sabha, addressing the ongoing discussions and debates on the introduced bills.

stories.headline: Union Home Minister Amit Shah said the BJP, if voted to power in West Bengal, will resolve the Gorkha issue in the Darjeeling hills in a constitutional manner
stories.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated that the BJP will resolve the Gorkha issue in Darjeeling hills in a constitutional manner without dividing West Bengal if voted to power. Addressing a poll rally in Gangarampur, Shah referred to the BJP's election manifesto, which includes development initiatives for north Bengal. He also promised to end political violence, syndicate, and cut-money raj in the state and evict infiltrators. Additionally, Shah pledged to include the Rajbanshi language in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution. The BJP's plan aims to address the long-standing Gorkha issue while maintaining the state's integrity. Shah's announcement comes as part of the BJP's campaign in north Bengal, where the party is seeking to gain support ahead of the elections. The resolution of the Gorkha issue is a key aspect of the BJP's election promises for West Bengal.

stories.headline: BJP-led Centre's push to add extra seats in Parliament is pivoted on reserving 33% of the seats for women
stories.summary: The BJP-led Centre has introduced a bill to reserve 33% of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and state legislatures, potentially effective from the 2029 general elections. This move aims to increase women's representation in Parliament, currently at 14% of 543 lower house MPs, to roughly a third, aligning with global norms. India already reserves 33% seats for women in village councils and municipal corporations. The bill would fast-track a 2023 law mandating 33% reservation for women in Parliament and state legislatures, marking a significant shift in political representation since India's independence. However, concerns have been raised, with some demanding genuine public consultation and delinking reservation from delimitation to implement it immediately within the existing 543 seats.

stories.headline: Stock Market Today Live April 17: Stock to buy today: Uno Minda (₹1,109.70) Stock Market Today | Share Market Live Updates - Find here all the live updates related to Sensex, Nifty, BSE, NSE, share prices and Indian stock markets for 17
stories.summary: Uno Minda's stock price is currently at ₹1,109.70. The company's share price can be tracked live on the National Stock Exchange (NSE) and Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE). Uno Minda Ltd's market capitalization, price-to-earnings ratio, earnings per share, return on capital employed, and return on equity are key metrics for investors. The company's 52-week high and low, as well as its upside and downside, are also available. Investors can access real-time trades, market depth, and historical performance details to inform their trading decisions. Additionally, information on dividends, shareholding patterns, and analyst ratings can be found. Uno Minda's stock is being closely watched, with live updates and detailed analysis available to help investors make informed decisions. The company's financials and news are also being monitored for any updates that may impact the stock price.

stories.headline: Sensex falls 123 points, Nifty 50 ends below 24,200: Key highlights from the Indian stock market today The Sensex declined 123 points, or 0.16%, to end at 77,988.68, while the Nifty 50 ended with a loss of 35 points, or 0.14%, at 24,196.75.
stories.summary: The Sensex declined 123 points to 77,988.68, a 0.16% drop, while the Nifty 50 ended 35 points lower at 24,196.75, a 0.14% decrease. The decline was largely due to banking and auto stocks. Despite this, the mid and small-cap segments performed well, indicating positive broader market sentiment. The NSE Nifty 50 index settled at 24,196.75, down 34.55 points. The drop was attributed to profit-taking and volatility. Benchmark equity indices, including the BSE Sensex and NSE Nifty 50, closed lower on Thursday. The fall in banking stocks weighed down the market, but selective buying in other segments helped mitigate the losses. The Sensex and Nifty 50 ended off their highs, with the Nifty 50 closing below the 24,200 mark.

stories.headline: Stock market today (April 16, 2026): Which are the top gainers and losers in Nifty50 and BSE Sensex today? Check list India Business News: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended lower in a volatile session on Thursday as profit-taking in banking and financial stocks erased ea
stories.summary: The Indian stock market ended Thursday's session on April 16, 2026, with modest losses. The NIFTY 50 closed at 24,196.75, down 34.55 points or 0.14%, after touching its highest level since early March. The 30-share BSE Sensex fell 122.56 points, or 0.16%, to close at 77,988.68. Profit-taking in banking and financial stocks erased early gains after a sharp rally in the previous session. The session was volatile, with the indices failing to sustain early gains. Top gainers and losers were reported in Nifty50 and BSE Sensex, with various catalysts behind each move across large, mid, and small caps, as well as sector cues and trading activity. The market's performance was closely watched, with real-time updates available throughout the day.

stories.headline: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Friday Sensex and Nifty ended lower in a volatile session as profit booking in banking stocks erased early gains. Despite easing volatility and supportive global cues,
stories.summary: Sensex and Nifty ended lower in a volatile session due to profit booking in banking stocks, erasing early gains. Despite supportive global cues, caution persists due to geopolitical uncertainties. The benchmark indices had initially gained but weakness in banking and media stocks led to a decline. IT and metal stocks, however, showed gains. The rupee also recovered, but it was not enough to lift the market. Broader markets continued to outperform, but auto and banking stocks dragged the indices lower. The market's decline came amid expiry day profit booking and selling pressure. Technical indicators suggest a bullish trend in the near term, but the market remains cautious due to global uncertainties. The Indian markets will look to global cues and domestic trends to decide the stock market action on Friday.

stories.headline: Undefined Targets Expose Moniepoint Agents To Arbitrary Termination POS agents across Nigeria may be operating under performance expectations that are never clearly defined, raising concerns about transparency and fairness
stories.summary: Nigerian POS agents are operating under unclear performance expectations, making them vulnerable to arbitrary termination. Hundreds of thousands of agents act as human ATMs and financial service hubs, particularly in areas without traditional banks. The Central Bank of Nigeria introduced new regulations in October 2025, including an exclusivity rule that mandates POS agents to affiliate with only one principal institution from April 2026. This rule aims to enhance security, fairness, and transparency in agent banking operations. However, POS operators have expressed concerns that the policy could drive small fintech companies out of business and create monopolies. The Association of Mobile Money and Bank Agents in Nigeria has also raised concerns about the potential impact of the new policy on the industry. The unclear performance expectations and new regulations may affect the livelihoods of many POS agents across the country.

stories.headline: Burry buys the dip in Salesforce and other software stocks after sell-off "I do not believe the technical pressures brought on by the private credit/software debt issues are big enough to affect these stocks for much longer," he said.
stories.summary: Investor Michael Burry has purchased shares of Salesforce and other software stocks following a recent sell-off. Burry believes that technical pressures from private credit and software debt issues are not significant enough to continue impacting these stocks. He expressed confidence that the effects of these issues will be short-lived. Burry's move to buy the dip suggests he expects the stocks to rebound. His investment decision is based on his assessment that the current market conditions are not reflective of the companies' long-term potential. By investing in these stocks, Burry is betting on their future growth and recovery from the recent decline. His statement indicates a bullish outlook on the software sector, despite current challenges. Burry's investment strategy often involves contrarian approaches, and this move is consistent with that approach.

stories.headline: Canadian Companies Not ‘Dumb Enough’ to Use Surveillance Pricing Technology says Retail Analyst A national retail analyst doesn't believe that a ban on so-called surveillance pricing is necessary in Canada.
stories.summary: A national retail analyst believes a ban on surveillance pricing is unnecessary in Canada, stating that Canadian companies are not "dumb enough" to use the technology. Surveillance pricing involves using personal data to offer individual customers different prices. The federal NDP has introduced a motion to ban the practice, citing concerns over how companies use personal data to set prices. Hotels, airlines, and ride-shares already use surge pricing, adjusting prices based on high demand. The debate over surveillance pricing is growing in Canada, with some consumers expressing concern over the use of their personal data to determine prices. The federal push to ban the practice has sparked discussion, including among Alberta consumers, after a previous attempt to ban it failed in Parliament.

stories.headline: EasyJet flight halted before takeoff as weight limits trigger last UK News: Safety concern halts flight from Southend to Malaga An EasyJet flight from London Southend Airport to Malaga delayed its departure on Saturday morning.
stories.summary: An EasyJet flight from London Southend Airport to Malaga was halted before takeoff on Saturday morning due to safety concerns. The crew determined that the aircraft exceeded safe operating weight under prevailing conditions, causing safety margins to dip below acceptable levels. To resolve the issue, crews recalculated the weight limits while passengers waited on board. The 08:40 BST service required a reduction in load, resulting in five passengers being asked to step off the plane just minutes before departure. The flight was delayed as a result of the safety precaution, which was taken to ensure the aircraft could operate within safe parameters. The incident highlights the airline's commitment to prioritizing safety and adhering to strict operating guidelines. The flight eventually departed after the necessary adjustments were made.
